info.name: XML
info.comment: Provides an interface for the management of structured data.

description.p: The XML module provides comprehensive support for XML 1.0 document parsing, manipulation, and querying. It integrates with the XPath module to deliver a standards-compliant XML processing environment with extensive querying capabilities.

The module implements full XML 1.0 parsing and serialisation, including support for namespaces, attributes, CDATA sections, processing instructions, and DOCTYPE declarations. Documents can be loaded from files, strings, or streams, and modified programmatically through a tree-based API. The parser validates well-formedness and provides detailed error reporting for malformed documents.
All aspects of XPath 2.0 are supported except for the following:

list.li: Namespace axis: The namespace::* and namespace::prefix axis expressions are not supported.
Schema imports: Schema import declarations are not recognised.
External variables: External variable declarations (declare variable $name external) are not supported.
Custom collations: Only the W3C codepoint collation (http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/collation/codepoint) is supported. Custom collation URIs are rejected.

description.p: The module implements core XQuery 1.0 functionality, including FLWOR expressions (for, let, where, order by, return, group by, count clauses), node constructors (element, attribute, document, text, comment, processing instruction), and a comprehensive function library covering strings, numbers, sequences, dates, durations, QNames, and document access. XQuery support excludes the following:

list.li: Schema-aware processing: Type validation against XML Schema is not supported.

function.name: XValueNodes
function.comment: For node-set XPathValue objects, returns the node-set as an array.
function.prototype: ERR xml::XValueNodes(struct XPathValue * Value, kt::vector<XTag *> * Result)

input.param: The node-set is returned here as an array of XTag structures.

description.p: If an XPathValue represents a node-set (type XPVT::NODE_SET) then XValueToNodes() will return a direct pointer to the node-set array.
Note: The integrity of the array is not guaranteed if the original XML document is modified or freed.

result.error: No data is available for use
A mis-match has been detected that prevents further processing

function.name: XValueToNumber
function.comment: Converts an XPathValue to a 64-bit floating point value.
function.prototype: ERR xml::XValueToNumber(struct XPathValue * Value, DOUBLE * Result)

input.param: The numeric representation of the value is returned here.

description.p: Call XValueToNumber() to convert an XPathValue object to a 64-bit floating point number. This function also includes cover support for boolean types, converting true to 1.0 and false to 0.

result.error: No data is available for use

function.name: XValueToString
function.comment: Converts an XPathValue to its string representation.
function.prototype: ERR xml::XValueToString(const struct XPathValue * Value, STRING * Result)

input.param: Receives the string representation of the value.

description.p: Call XValueToString() to convert an XPathValue object into its string representation.

result.error: No data is available for use

constants.const: Tag represents a comment of the format <!-- Comment -->.
Tag represents an instruction of the format <?xml?>.
Tag represents a notation of the format <!XML>.

struct.name: XPathValue
struct.field: Identifies the type of value stored
Defined if the type is Number or Boolean
Defined if the type is String

struct.name: XTag
struct.field: Globally unique ID assigned to the tag on creation.
Line number on which this tag was encountered
Hash of namespace URI or 0 for no namespace
Array of attributes for this tag
